title: UPS Battery Failure / Power Alert
symptoms: UPS switches to battery mode — mains power lost
Battery health warning or battery test failure
UPS runtime estimate drops below safe threshold (<10 minutes)
SNMP trap from UPS management card
steps: Identify the affected UPS unit and its load: check UPS management card web UI or NMS
Confirm whether this is a mains power failure or a UPS hardware fault
Check battery runtime remaining on UPS card — if <15 minutes, begin controlled shutdown procedure
Check if the site has a generator — verify it has started and is taking load
Contact the facilities/power team or building management for mains status
Check if other devices on the same PDU are affected
Review battery health report: date of last replacement, number of discharge cycles
If UPS fault (not mains): check UPS bypass mode availability before shutting down
resolution: If mains failure: engage generator or coordinate with power utility for ETA. If battery is degraded: schedule emergency battery replacement within 24 hours. If UPS unit is faulty: switch to bypass, protect downstream gear, and arrange UPS service call. Notify datacenter manager of all power events.
escalation: If runtime drops below 10 minutes with no generator or mains recovery, initiate controlled datacenter shutdown sequence per DR plan. Page datacenter manager and NOC manager immediately.
estimated_resolution_time: Mains restore: 30-120 minutes. Battery replacement: 4-8 hours.
reference: Emircom DC Power & UPS Management SOP — Rev 5.0
